2604.10166.title: Virtual Smart Metering in District Heating Networks via Heterogeneous Spatial-Temporal Graph Neural Networks
2604.10166.paper_summary: Intelligent operation of thermal energy networks aims to improve energy efficiency, reliability, and operational flexibility through data-driven control, predictive optimization, and early fault detection. Achieving these goals relies on sufficient observability, requiring continuous and well-distributed monitoring of thermal and hydraulic states. However, district heating systems are typically sparsely instrumented and frequently affected by sensor faults, limiting monitoring. Virtual sensing offers a cost-effective means to enhance observability, yet its development and validation remain limited in practice. Existing data-driven methods generally assume dense synchronized data, while analytical models rely on simplified hydraulic and thermal assumptions that may not adequately capture the behavior of heterogeneous network topologies. Consequently, modeling the coupled nonlinear dependencies between pressure, flow, and temperature under realistic operating conditions remains challenging. In addition, the lack of publicly available benchmark datasets hinders systematic comparison of virtual sensing approaches. To address these challenges, we propose a heterogeneous spatial-temporal graph neural network (HSTGNN) for constructing virtual smart heat meters. The model incorporates the functional relationships inherent in district heating networks and employs dedicated branches to learn graph structures and temporal dynamics for flow, temperature, and pressure measurements, thereby enabling the joint modeling of cross-variable and spatial correlations. To support further research, we introduce a controlled laboratory dataset collected at the Aalborg Smart Water Infrastructure Laboratory, providing synchronized high-resolution measurements representative of real operating conditions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly outperforms existing baselines.
2604.10166.paper_summary_zh: 智能運行熱能網絡旨在通過數據驅動控制、預測優化和早期故障檢測來提高能效、可靠性和運營靈活性。實現這些目標依賴於足夠的可觀測性，這需要對熱和液壓狀態進行持續且良好分佈的監測。然而，區域供熱系統通常儀器稀少，且經常受到傳感器故障的影響，限制了監測。虛擬感測提供了一種成本效益高的方式來增強可觀測性，但其開發和驗證在實踐中仍然有限。現有的數據驅動方法通常假設密集的同步數據，而分析模型則依賴於簡化的液壓和熱假設，這可能無法充分捕捉異質網絡拓撲的行為。因此，在現實操作條件下建模壓力、流量和溫度之間的耦合非線性依賴關係仍然具有挑戰性。此外，缺乏公開可用的基準數據集妨礙了虛擬感測方法的系統比較。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了一種異質時空圖神經網絡（HSTGNN）來構建虛擬智能熱量計。該模型納入了區域供熱網絡固有的功能關係，並利用專門的分支來學習流量、溫度和壓力測量的圖結構和時間動態，從而實現跨變量和空間相關性的聯合建模。為了支持進一步的研究，我們引入了一個在奧爾堡智能水基礎設施實驗室收集的受控實驗室數據集，提供代表真實操作條件的同步高解析度測量。大量實驗表明，所提出的方法顯著優於現有基準。
2604.10166.author: Keivan Faghih Niresi et.al.
2604.10166.authors: Keivan Faghih Niresi, Christian Møller Jensen, Carsten Skovmose Kallesøe, Rafael Wisniewski, Olga Fink
